raw.title: How environmental laws are shifting the focus from humans to nature
raw.excerpt: Environmental laws are evolving from prioritizing human benefits to recognizing nature’s intrinsic rights; this is reflected in the small but growing number of countries that grant legal personhood to natural entities.

llm_processed.summary: The article traces how environmental laws are evolving to prioritize ecological systems over human-centric protections, drawing on historical examples from ancient civilizations.
llm_processed.lateral_potential: Bridges to policy design that integrates ecological values into legal frameworks, supporting regenerative systems.
